UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user profile within the ZYRA system, including authentication-related identifiers and gamification statistics. Authentication details like passwords are not stored here.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity (matches Firebase Auth UID).

name.description: The full name of the user.

role.description: The role of the user within the system, defining their permissions.

level.description: The current gamification level of the user.

points.description: The total gamification points accumulated by the user.

streak.description: The number of consecutive days the user has submitted a report.

achievementIds.description: References to Achievements obtained by the user. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N Achievement)

teamId.description: Reference to the current Team the user belongs to. (Relationship: Team 1:N UserProfile)

Project.title: Project
Project.description: Represents a solar energy installation project managed by ZYRA, detailing its status and location.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Project entity.

client.description: The name of the client for whom the project is being executed.

status.description: The current operational status of the project (e.g., execution, validation, finalized).

description.description: A detailed description of the project.

location.description: The geographical location of the project. Represented as a string for database agnosticism, often a Geopoint string representation or a composite object in specific databases.

Team.title: Team
Team.description: Represents a team of employees working on ZYRA projects, including its leader and assigned project.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Team entity.

leaderId.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the team's leader. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Team)

memberIds.description: References to UserProfiles that are members of this team. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N Team)

assignedProjectId.description: Reference to the Project currently assigned to this team. (Relationship: Project 1:N Team)

Report.title: Report
Report.description: Represents an operational report submitted by an employee for a specific project.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Report entity.

date.description: The date and time when the report was submitted.

employeeId.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the employee who submitted the report. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Report)

projectId.description: Reference to the Project related to this report. (Relationship: Project 1:N Report)

content.description: The textual content of the report, which may also be a URL to an image stored in Firebase Storage.

pointsAwarded.description: The number of gamification points awarded for this report submission.

Achievement.title: Achievement
Achievement.description: Represents a catalog entry for an achievement that users can earn, contributing to gamification.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Achievement entity.

icon.description: An emoji or a URL to an image representing the achievement's icon.

description.description: A detailed description of how to earn the achievement.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The `id` field matches the Firebase Auth UID for direct ownership. Includes `teamId` for team association and `achievementIds` for gamification tracking. The `role` field within this document is used for both application logic and security rule authorization.

Contains details about solar energy installation projects. To ensure authorization independence and queryable access patterns (QAPs) for employee access, each project document **must include a denormalized `assignedTeamId` field** (this field is not explicitly present in the provided `Project` schema but is critical for rule checks without hierarchical dependencies on the `teams` collection). This `assignedTeamId` will match the `id` of a `Team` document.

Manages teams of employees, their leader, and members. The `memberIds` array directly supports membership checks. `assignedProjectId` links the team to its current project. Employee read access is based on comparing their `teamId` (obtained from their `UserProfile` document) with the `teamId` in the document path.

Stores operational reports submitted by employees. Includes `employeeId` for direct ownership-based authorization and `projectId` for contextual linking. Supports secure write operations ensuring `request.auth.uid` matches the report's `employeeId`.
A global catalog of gamification achievements. This collection is publicly readable by all authenticated users and managed by administrators.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for ZYRA Command is designed to prioritize authorization independence, clear security rules, and efficient querying, adhering strictly to the provided design principles.

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization (Strategy A):**
1.  **User Roles:** User roles (`admin`, `employee`) are stored in the `role` field within each `UserProfile` document (`/userProfiles/{userProfileId}`). While this means security rules for other collections will perform a `get()` operation on the *requesting user's own profile* (`get(/userProfiles/$(request.auth.uid)).data.role`), this is an accepted pattern. It directly ties authorization to the authenticated user's immutable ID (`request.auth.uid`) and their own stored attributes, avoiding hierarchical dependencies on the *target resource's* parent or unrelated documents. This adheres to DBAC (roles in database, auth on `request.auth.uid`).
2.  **Project-Team Assignment:** The `Project` entity (`/projects/{projectId}`) *must* include a denormalized `assignedTeamId` field (this field is not explicitly in the provided `Project` schema but is critical for secure, scalable access). This enables direct checks in security rules to determine if an employee is authorized to read a specific project. This denormalization eliminates the need for `get()` operations on the `/teams` collection when evaluating project access.
3.  **Report Ownership:** The `Report` entity (`/reports/{reportId}`) directly includes `employeeId`. This allows for straightforward, independent ownership checks (`request.auth.uid == request.resource.data.employeeId` for create/update, `request.auth.uid == resource.data.employeeId` for read) without any `get()` calls.
4.  **Team Membership:** The `Team` entity (`/teams/{teamId}`) includes a `memberIds` array. This directly holds authorization context for members. Employee read access to their own team is facilitated by checking their `teamId` from their own user profile against the `teamId` in the path.

**Structural Segregation (Strategy B):**
All collections maintain a homogeneous security posture: `/userProfiles`, `/projects`, `/teams`, `/reports`, `/achievements` are top-level. Each document within these collections shares the same general access patterns (e.g., all projects have similar access logic, all reports do too). No mixing of data with different access requirements.

**Queryable Access Patterns (QAPs - Rules are not Filters):**
This structure directly supports QAPs, allowing efficient and secure `list` operations:
*   **User Profiles:** Employees can only query their own profile (`db.collection('userProfiles').doc(request.auth.uid)`). Admins can query all.
*   **Projects:** Employees can query projects assigned to their team (`db.collection('projects').where('assignedTeamId', '==', currentUserTeamId)`). The rule verifies `currentUserTeamId` matches the authenticated user's `teamId` from their profile. Admins can query all.
*   **Teams:** Employees can query their own team (`db.collection('teams').doc(currentUserTeamId)`). The rule verifies `currentUserTeamId` against the user's profile. Admins can query all.
*   **Reports:** Employees can query their own reports (`db.collection('reports').where('employeeId', '==', request.auth.uid)`). Admins can query all.
*   **Achievements:** All authenticated users can query the entire catalog.
